essence: David wants agent memory to support selectable/configurable modes — pointing at a plain folder vs watching a separate 'actual brain' folder — with one-button on/off UX and the behavior well documented.

actions.text: Document selectable memory modes (plain folder vs watched brain) and their on/off configuration

essence: Amid TV background audio, David asked that after the delivery review runs he get a quality comparison of Anthropic's built-in code review versus his delivery-review skill — are they redundant, complementary, or something else.

actions.text: Compare Anthropic built-in code review output against the delivery-review skill for redundancy vs complementarity

loop.label: code review vs delivery review comparison

essence: Steering a KyberBot session over TV background, David confirmed the 375 records were reconciled via KBDE, asked for a shutdown script when closing Kyber Agent Enterprise, and requested a 2-minute progress loop tracking the baseline run's done/remaining/ETA.

actions.text: Create a shutdown script that runs when closing Kyber Agent Enterprise
Set up a 2-minute progress loop reporting baseline run done/remaining/ETA

essence: With the Kyber enterprise environment shut down for direct database access, David checked whether the master-branch code change was safe to push, challenged a stale 4:57 launch report, questioned whether the backup database is still needed, and asked for a ticket with a URL to paste into another system.

actions.text: Write up a ticket for the backup-database cleanup question and provide its URL

essence: Preparing to move to a fresh window, David asked for a handover message, ordered the background task stopped with no changes on main, and wanted probes and outstanding tasks from the last session reviewed — frustrated that a new window carried no knowledge of remaining work.

actions.text: Draft a handover message and revive the outstanding probe tasks in the new session

essence: For the fourth session running, KyberBot Desktop reports 'No files being watched' for the KyberBrain sources and the agent retains no memory of the prior three reports — David is angry the recurring bug is neither remembered nor fixed.

actions.text: Diagnose and permanently fix the 'No files being watched' sources bug

essence: David asked whether any written record exists of the last four sessions where the watching issue was raised — probing whether the agent persists session knowledge anywhere durable.

At ~75% token usage David called the agent's memory-based recall partially wrong (fourth recurrence) and demanded persistence more durable than memories, while asking how the 121-missing KBDE reconciliation's progress can surface in the KyberBot Desktop app and how to redo probes with a clean test set.

actions.text: Persist session learnings somewhere more durable than agent memories
Surface reconciliation progress (done/remaining) in the desktop application
Define how to redo probes with a clean test set

essence: David requested a gap analysis of Remotion vs Hyperframes (what each can do, why one is better) plus a background-agent proof of concept turning four slides into a video with music/interactivity, while also queuing brain re-ingestion via KBDE for the 375 wrong files and documentation of study-setting defaults and probe findings.

actions.text: Run a gap analysis between Remotion and Hyperframes
Investigate a background-agent POC turning the four slides into a video
Document study-setting defaults, on/off rationale, and probe findings

essence: David questioned why the brain-related folder is stored under remotion instead of video-as-code, asked whether missing reconciliation values should be fixed, and directed the video-as-code/hyperframes brain content be committed and pushed.

actions.text: Commit and push the video-as-code / hyperframes brain-related content

essence: Closing out a session, David flagged that fresh snapshots/database clones (taken to avoid conflicts with the running KyberBot desktop agent) are burning disk space and the agent should instead detect and shut the desktop agent down, asked to locate the Fable 5 video session and Angela's Supporting Potential story session folders for claude resume patterns, and wanted loop-engineering notes routed into dark-factory and the brains with the hyperframes work committed and pushed.

actions.text: Stop snapshot/database-clone disk blowout — detect and shut down the desktop agent instead of cloning to dodge write locks
Locate folders and session IDs for the Fable 5 video session and Angela's Supporting Potential session to build resume commands
Route loop-engineering learnings into dark-factory + brains and commit/push the hyperframes brain updates
